name: Tokenization
tagline: Turns raw text into discrete symbols for language models
problem:
Text is not naturally a fixed-size numeric input. Models need a stable representation that is efficient, reversible enough for decoding, and compatible with training.

Tokenization solves this by mapping text to token IDs. Poor tokenization inflates sequence length, harms multilingual performance, and creates brittle behavior around punctuation and whitespace.

The remaining problem for Tokenization is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats tokenization as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Modern tokenizers often use subword methods (BPE/Unigram) to balance vocabulary size against sequence length. The tokenizer becomes part of the model contract: changing it changes what the model can express and how it generalizes.

For bootstrap, tokenization is attractive because it is reproducible without exotic hardware; the limiting factors are correct implementation and careful evaluation across languages and domains.

In collapse recovery, tokenization must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Compression vs expressivity
principles.explanation: Smaller vocabularies increase sequence length; larger vocabularies increase sparsity and memory.

principles.name: Reversibility
principles.explanation: Decoding should reconstruct text deterministically to preserve meaning.

principles.name: Domain coverage
principles.explanation: Vocab must handle technical symbols, units, and scripts used by your corpus.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Tokenization is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Representative text corpus
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Use locally relevant corpora: technical manuals, logs, and training materials stored offline.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Deduplicate, filter, and normalize; keep licensing and provenance records.

buildSteps.title: Define corpus and normalization
buildSteps.description: Choose what text represents your domain; define normalization rules up front.
buildSteps.warningNote: Normalization changes meaning if done carelessly.

buildSteps.title: Train tokenizer
buildSteps.description: Train BPE/Unigram tokenizer; choose vocab size by evaluation, not aesthetics.
buildSteps.warningNote: Too-small vocab explodes sequence lengths and compute.

buildSteps.title: Evaluate edge cases
buildSteps.description: Test with units, code, multilingual text, and punctuation-heavy content.
buildSteps.warningNote: Tokenizer bugs become permanent model behavior.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Tokenization is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

buildSteps.title: Inspect inputs before use
buildSteps.description: Check materials, tools, water, power, workspace, records, and operator readiness. Mark substitutions and unknowns before work begins.
buildSteps.warningNote: Substitutions can introduce contamination, fire, structural, electrical, medical, or chemical hazards.

history.event: Statistical NLP uses word-level and character models.
Subword tokenization becomes standard for neural NLP.
Tokenizer design becomes a core scaling decision.

impact:
Tokenization shapes what a language model can learn and how efficiently it trains. It is a hidden but critical piece of the AI stack.

In The Codex, tokenization is a gateway node between raw text archives and large-scale transformers.

images.caption: Text, normalization, vocabulary, token IDs, decoding

sources.title: Sennrich et al. (2016) — Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
